messages.content:
Which of the following is a target of UN SDG 15? 

A.
Promote Mechanisms to Raise Capacity for Climate Planning and Management

B.
Conserve Coastal and Marine Areas 

C.
Ensure Equal Opportunities and End Discrimination 

D.
Protect Biodiversity and Natural Habitants
D. Protect Biodiversity and Natural Habitants

What is the UN SDG 15?

A.
Life Below Water 

B.
Responsible Consumption and Production 

C.
Climate Action

D.
Life on Land
D. Life on Land

What is the goal of UN SDG 13? 

A.
Taking gradual action to tackle climate change and its impacts

B.
Taking urgent action to tackle climate change and its impacts 

C.
Taking strategic action to tackle climate change and its impacts

D.
Taking haphazard action to tackle climate change and its impacts
B. Taking urgent action to tackle climate change and its impacts

How many are the indicators of UN SDG 13? 

A.
16

B.
6 

C.
11

D.
8

What is the UN SDG 13? 

A.
Climate Action 

B.
Sustainable Cities and Communities 

C.
Life below Water

D.
Life on Land
A. Climate Action

How many parties ratified the Paris Agreement as of April 2018? 

A.
176

B.
175

C.
185

D.
186

How many are the targets of UN SDG 15?

A.
11

B.
8 

C.
.12

D.
14

Which of the following is NOT a target of UN SDG 14? 

A.
Integrate Climate Change Measures into Policies and Planning

B.
Reduce Marine Pollution 

C.
Sustainable Fishing 

D.
Increase Scientific Knowledge, Research and Technology for Ocean Health
A. Integrate Climate Change Measures into Policies and Planning

Why is addressing the problems of the world’s bodies of water and marine resources important? 

A.
Our rainwater, drinking water, weather, climate, coastlines, much of our food, and even the oxygen in the air we breathe, are all ultimately regulated by the sea.

B.
Our rainwater, drinking water, weather, climate, coastlines, much of our food, and even the oxygen in the air we breathe, are all ultimately provided and regulated by the sea.

C.
ur rainwater, drinking water, weather, climate, coastlines, much of our food, and even the oxygen in the air we breathe, are all ultimately suppressedby the sea.

D.
Our rainwater, drinking water, weather, climate, coastlines, much of our food, and even the oxygen in the air we breathe, are all ultimately provided by the sea.
B. Our rainwater, drinking water, weather, climate, coastlines, much of our food, and even the oxygen in the air we breathe, are all ultimately provided and regulated by the sea.
How do you define SDG 16: Peace, Justice & Strong Institutions

SDG 16: Peace, Justice & Strong Institutions is one of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. It aims to promote peaceful and inclusive societies, provide access to justice for all, and build effective, accountable, and inclusive institutions at all levels. This goal recognizes the importance of strong governance, rule of law, and institutions that uphold human rights and promote peaceful coexistence. It also seeks to reduce violence, combat corruption, and ensure equal access to justice for all individuals.
